question: why did checkout start failing around 3am? customers are seeing 5xx on payment submit.

trajectory.stdout_excerpt: services: vault, payment-gateway, checkout-worker, api-gateway, redis; level values: info, warn, error

error#1 (312): payment-gateway returned 401 (checkout-worker)
error#2 (87+142+168+156): auth rejected surge (payment-gateway)
payment-gateway warn auth rejected presented_version=6 expected_version=7 caller=checkout-worker
vault info secret rotated old_version=6 new_version=7; webhook dispatched to payment-gateway; checkout-worker NOT subscribed

report.root_cause: checkout-worker is still presenting stripe_api_key version 6 to payment-gateway after vault rotated the secret to version 7 at 02:58:04 UTC. payment-gateway subscribed to the rotation webhook and flipped to v7 instantly, with zero grace period for v6. checkout-worker did not subscribe and caches the secret in-process with no SIGHUP handler, so every outbound charge signed with v6 is now rejected 401 invalid_api_key_version. retries re-present the same stale secret, cascading to api-gateway 502 and a redis queue backup that paged oncall as user-facing 5xx.
report.blast_radius: 47 minutes (02:58:41 to 03:45:30 UTC). ~4,120 checkout sessions affected, ~612 failed orders, estimated $58k revenue impact. recovery came from a rolling restart of checkout-worker pods at 03:45 which forced a fresh vault fetch of v7.

evidence.why: the rotation is the precipitating event

evidence.text_redacted: auth rejected reason=invalid_api_key_version presented=6 expected=7 caller=checkout-worker auth=[REDACTED_BEARER]
evidence.why: first rejection tying checkout-worker to the stale key version
caller side of the 401, same trace_id
user-visible impact, this is what paged oncall

evidence.text_redacted: queue lag threshold exceeded queue=checkout.submit depth=1104
evidence.why: secondary symptom, confirms dequeue collapsed at the same time 401s started

report.remediation: already applied: rolling restart of checkout-worker forced re-read of stripe_api_key v7 from vault. permanent fix: (1) subscribe checkout-worker to the vault rotation webhook so it refreshes in-process secrets within 30s of rotation, (2) add a SIGHUP handler that reloads secrets without restart, (3) have payment-gateway honor a short grace window (60s) where v(N-1) is still accepted, to absorb clock skew between subscribers.
report.confidence_rationale: the causal chain is fully covered by the logs: rotation event at 02:58:04, first 401 at 02:58:41, recovery immediately after pod restart at 03:45. not 1.0 because we do not directly observe the in-memory secret version inside checkout-worker, we infer it from the presented_version field payment-gateway reports.
unknowns: exact number of failed customer sessions (we see failed orders in checkout-worker but not unique session IDs)
whether any partial charges leaked through before payment-gateway cutover at 02:58:05
